title: Why Your Plants Need Terracotta Pots More Than Ever During The Rains
excerpt: The monsoon brings beauty but also the danger of root rot. Discover why the natural, breathable power of terracotta is your plant's best defense against the humidity and dampness of the rainy season.

content.value: When the monsoon arrives in Sri Lanka, it brings a much needed break from the scorching sun. But while we are enjoying our tea and watching the downpour, our indoor and balcony plants might be struggling. The rainy season presents a unique set of challenges for plant parents: lower light, high humidity, and the constant threat of overwatering.
This is the time of year when your choice of planter becomes a matter of life or death for your green friends.
The Humidity Factor

In the rainy season, the air is thick with moisture. This means that water in the soil does not evaporate nearly as fast as it does during the dry months. If your plants are sitting in plastic or glazed ceramic pots, the soil can stay swampy for weeks.

Terracotta is naturally breathable. The porous clay acts as a secondary drainage system, allowing moisture to escape through the walls of the pot. At Terra by Shavindi, we craft our pots to ensure this natural airflow remains a priority. This helps the soil dry out at a healthy pace, even when the humidity is at ninety percent.
Preventing The Dreaded Root Rot

Root rot is the number one killer of indoor plants during the monsoon. Because there is less sunlight to help the plant "drink" water, the roots often sit in stagnant moisture. Without oxygen, the roots begin to decay, and by the time you see yellowing leaves, it is often too late.

A terracotta pot provides a safety net. It wicks away the excess water that the plant cannot use, keeping the root zone aerated. It is the most effective, low-tech way to prevent fungal infections and rot during the wettest months of the year.
Stable Temperatures For Sensitive Roots

Rainy days often come with sudden drops in temperature. While we might find the chill refreshing, tropical plants can be sensitive to "cold feet." Terracotta is an incredible insulator. The thick clay walls provide a stable environment for the root system, protecting it from the rapid temperature fluctuations that happen when a thunderstorm rolls in.
A Natural Aesthetic For Gloomy Days

There is something deeply comforting about the warm, orange-hued tones of terracotta when the sky is grey. Our minimal and boho designs at Terra by Shavindi are intended to bring a sense of groundedness to your space.

While plastic pots can look stark and cold in the dim light of a rainy afternoon, terracotta adds a layer of earthy warmth. It pairs perfectly with the vibrant greens of rain-soaked leaves, making your indoor garden feel like a cozy sanctuary rather than a damp corner.
Tips For Rainy Day Plant Care
If you are transitioning your plants to terracotta this season, here are a few things to keep in mind:
Check the Soil:
Even with terracotta, always poke your finger an inch into the soil before watering.
Clear the Saucers:
Do not let your terracotta pots sit in a pool of water in their saucers; empty them regularly to keep the air flowing.
Group Your Pots:
Grouping your terracotta planters together can help create a tiny, healthy micro-climate for your plants.
Invest In Your Garden’s Health
The rainy season should be a time of growth, not a time of loss. By switching to artisanal terracotta, you are giving your plants the best chance to thrive in the tropical humidity.
Explore the collection at Terra by Shavindi to find the perfect, breathable home for your plants this monsoon. Your indoor jungle will thank you for it.
